factors.name: Real Yields & Treasury Selloff
factors.summary: US 10Y Treasury yield hit highest since 2023; 30Y yield at 3-year highs. Japan 10Y hit 1996 highs at 4.2%. Global bond rout deepens as inflation fears mount from Iran war oil supply shock. Long-duration growth stocks (Nasdaq 100) highly sensitive to real rate moves — rising yields compress valuations.

factors.name: Oil Shock & Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: WTI at 2-week highs (~$107); Brent ~$110. Strait of Hormuz blockade persists, UAE nuclear facility drone strike escalates tensions. US oil inventories near exhaustion (102M barrels). Copper extended retreat on inflation fears. This drives Fed tightening bias, which crushes multiple-dependent Nasdaq stocks.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations Shifting Hawkish
factors.summary: Yardeni Research warns Fed must 'drop easing bias or lose control of rates.' Heightened inflation concerns reinforce bets for higher interest rates. USD/JPY at 158.90 on hawkish Fed bets. This reprices growth equities lower as terminal rate expectations rise.

factors.name: China Economic Shock
factors.summary: China April retail sales +0.2% y/y (expected 2.0%, prior 1.7%) — weakest since 2022. Industrial production +4.1% y/y (expected 5.9%, prior 5.7%). Fixed asset investment -1.6% y/d ytd (expected 1.7%). House prices -3.5% y/y. External demand weakness + internal deterioration affects semiconductor/tech capex cycle.

factors.name: Risk-Off Sentiment in Asia
factors.summary: South Korea KOSPI futures hit circuit-breaker (down 5%); volatility near record highs after $13.2B foreign investor selloff last week. AUD/USD, NZD/USD, GBP/USD all weakening on geopolitical risk. CNY under pressure (PBOC set USD/CNY at 6.8435, allowing depreciation). Broad risk-off mood.

factors.name: Geopolitical Escalation (Iran War)
factors.summary: Trump warns Iran 'clock is ticking' or 'there won't be anything left.' White House situation room meeting scheduled for Tuesday, May 19 to discuss military options. Drone attacks on UAE nuclear facility. This raises tail risk of war escalation, oil spike, and flight to safety out of equities.

factors.name: Semiconductor Supply Chain Uncertainty
factors.summary: ARM Holdings faces US antitrust probe per Bloomberg. Samsung union wage talks resumed (shares jumped initially but broader sector headwind). CXMT IPO positive for memory demand, but macro uncertainty overshadows. Mixed signal; not yet a clear negative, but geopolitical risk dampens capex outlook.

factors.name: AI Capex & Hyperscaler Demand (Positive Offset)
factors.summary: Singapore April non-oil exports beat badly at +24.5% y/y (vs 10.9% expected), driven by robust AI-related electronics and semiconductors demand. Billionaire Gina Rinehart invested $100M in US defense stocks. Some pockets of AI momentum remain intact, but macro headwinds dominate.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD broadly stronger on hawkish Fed repricing and energy supply shock insulation (US is net oil exporter). DXY should benefit, which typically pressures international-revenue-heavy Nasdaq mega-caps (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL have ~50%+ FX exposure). Headwind for NAS100.

factors.name: Price Action — Minor Downside Move in Progress
factors.summary: 4h change: -0.67%, 24h change: -0.22%. Small but directional. Move appears to be Phase 2 — not yet extended, but accumulating on the downside through Sunday into Monday Asia open. Positioning likely neutral-to-long into weekend; reversal lower is attracting selling on opens.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a fresh bear trap driven by a triple shock: (1) global bond selloff on surging inflation fears from the Iran war oil supply crunch (10Y yields at 3-year highs), (2) China's April data collapse (retail sales +0.2%, lowest since 2022; IP miss 4.1% vs 5.9% expected) signaling external and internal demand deterioration, and (3) hawkish Fed repricing as traders price in persistent inflation and rate-hike continuation. Real yields rising sharply are the single largest headwind for long-duration growth equities. Geopolitical escalation risk (Trump's Tuesday military meeting, drone strikes on UAE nuclear plant) adds tail-risk premium. The move down is still in Phase 2 (not yet extended), with Asia selling into Monday's open on both the China data miss and oil-driven inflation spike.
catalysts_ahead: Trump White House Situation Room meeting Tue, May 19 (military options discussion on Iran)
Japanese GDP Q1 (Tue) — expected to show Iran war energy headwinds
NVDA earnings (likely this week) — forward AI capex guidance critical
US CPI / Core CPI (likely Fri, May 23) — will set inflation narrative for Fed
RBA Minutes (Tue) — central bank inflation response
UK jobs data & EZ trade balance (Tue) — echo China weakness into developed economies
Oil inventory levels (weekly EIA) — supply shock severity
screening.risk_warning: BIAS INVALIDATION: If Iran ceasefire is announced Tuesday or Wednesday, oil drops >5%, real yields fall sharply, and tech reprices higher. If China stimulus is announced (any RRR cut, fiscal package) and data rebound accelerates demand expectations, multiple-compression trade stops. If Fed pivots dovish in response to credit stress, equities rally hard. If NVDA earnings beat massively and raise FY26 capex guidance, AI exuberance could override macro headwinds (short-term). Monitor crude oil intraday — break below $105 WTI would reduce stagflation fears and ease pressure.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2; wait for retest of 29,200–29,300 resistance on any intraday bounce before shorting or scaling in. If breaks 28,900 support decisively on close, Phase 3 acceleration may follow. Avoid chasing down at open; better risk/reward on pullback entries into intermediate resistance into Tuesday's geopolitical headline risk.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Surging
factors.summary: US 30-year yield hits highest since 2023, global bond rout deepening on inflation fears (Bloomberg). Real yields rising sharply = massive compression for long-duration tech multiples. Japan 10Y at 1996 high (4.2%), fiscal concerns spreading.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations Shifting Hawkish
factors.summary: Yardeni urges Fed to drop easing bias or lose control of rates (Bloomberg). Hawkish Fed bets driving USD higher (FXStreet). Market repricing away from cuts toward potential hikes on inflation concerns = headwind for growth stocks.

factors.name: Iran War Stalemate & Oil Shock
factors.summary: Oil touching 2-week high after UAE nuclear plant drone attack, WTI ~$107, Brent ~$110. Trump warns Iran 'clock is ticking' with Situation Room meeting Tuesday to discuss military options (Axios). Strait of Hormuz blockade persisting, global oil stocks near exhaustion = sustained inflation pressure.

factors.name: China Economic Data Miss
factors.summary: China April retail sales +0.2% vs +2.0% exp (worst since 2022), industrial production +4.1% vs +5.9% exp, fixed asset investment -1.6% (China NBS). Major demand signal for semiconductors and tech hardware - Apple, NVDA exposure to China weakness.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: Ebola outbreak declared public health emergency (WHO/MarketWatch), Korea volatility near record after $13B foreign selloff, KOSPI futures hit limit down triggering exchange halt. Global risk-off environment hitting growth assets hardest.

factors.name: Semiconductor Supply Chain Positives
factors.summary: CXMT (Chinese chipmaker) reports 8x revenue jump ahead of IPO (Bloomberg), Samsung union talks resume averting strike (shares +5%), Singapore non-oil exports +24.5% driven by strong electronics/IC demand. AI demand tailwind partially offsetting macro.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD broadly firmer on hawkish Fed repricing and energy insulation (RBC notes US net oil exporter status shields dollar). NDX components have heavy international revenue exposure - stronger dollar = earnings headwind.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations Rising
factors.summary: Copper retreating on inflation fears tied to Iran war (Bloomberg), gold declining as real yields spike. Market pricing sustained inflation shock from energy = Fed stays higher for longer = growth multiple compression continues.

factors.name: NVDA Earnings This Week
factors.summary: NVDA earnings due (mentioned in 'retailer reports' context), single most important event for NDX direction. AI demand commentary will be critical - could override macro if guidance extremely strong, but setup is binary risk.

factors.name: Tech Regulation Overhang
factors.summary: Arm Holdings facing US antitrust probe over chip tech (Bloomberg). Adds to regulatory pressure on tech sector alongside ongoing DOJ/FTC scrutiny of mega-caps.

confirmation.narrative: NAS100 faces a confluence of bearish macro forces: surging real yields (30Y Treasury at 3-year high) compressing long-duration growth multiples, Iran war stalemate driving oil to $107 WTI with Trump threatening military escalation Tuesday, and Fed repricing hawkish on sustained inflation fears. China's shocking April data miss (retail +0.2% vs +2% exp, worst since 2022) signals demand weakness for tech exports. While semiconductor supply chain shows AI strength (CXMT 8x revenue jump, Samsung strike averted), the macro headwinds dominate. Current -0.67% 4h decline suggests move is underway but not extended.
catalysts_ahead: Trump Situation Room meeting Tuesday May 19 on Iran military options - major escalation risk
NVIDIA earnings this week - single biggest NDX driver, AI demand commentary critical
Canadian CPI Tuesday, Japanese Q1 GDP Tuesday, UK jobs data - inflation trajectory signals
RBA Minutes Tuesday, NZ Q1 PPI - regional central bank policy clues
Walmart & Target earnings - consumer spending under energy price pressure (gas $4.50-5.00 threshold)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if: (1) Iran breakthrough announced before Tuesday meeting, oil collapses below $95; (2) NVDA earnings massively beat with blowout AI guidance, overriding macro; (3) surprise dovish Fed speak or Treasury yield reversal below 4.5% on 10Y; (4) China announces major stimulus package. NVDA earnings are binary - could swing NDX ±3% regardless of macro setup.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway (-0.67% in 4h). Wait for bounce to 29250-29350 resistance zone to consider short entries, or break below 28950 for continuation confirmation. Avoid chasing here with NVDA earnings and Trump meeting looming - binary events ahead.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Real Rates Spike
factors.summary: Global bond yields surged Monday amid inflation fears; US Treasury yields broke March highs on geopolitical disruption (Strait of Hormuz closure, US-Iran stalemate). Higher nominal and real yields compress long-duration growth multiples critical to Nasdaq 100 valuations.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Inflation Shock
factors.summary: WTI near $102.70, Brent elevated on 12-week Strait of Hormuz closure; oil shock driving upstream CPI surprises. Stagflationary backdrop—higher energy costs pressure corporate margins while inflation expectations re-price Fed policy toward hawkish, not dovish.

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: US Dollar extended gains on Fed rate hike repricing and geopolitical safe-haven bid; EUR/USD at 6-week lows near 1.1620. Nasdaq 100 has ~40% international revenue exposure; stronger USD headwind for mega-caps' reported earnings.

factors.name: Risk-Off Sentiment & Equity Selloff
factors.summary: Asia-Pacific down (Nikkei -1.08%, ASX -1.45%), European stocks sliding, crypto liquidations ($563M), foreign investor dump in South Korea ($13.2B). Broad de-risking from growth/leverage positions as yields shock markets.

factors.name: China Economic Weakness
factors.summary: China April retail sales +0.2% y/y (weakest since 2022, vs 2% expected), industrial production +4.1% y/y (vs 5.9% expected). Demand backdrop deteriorating; affects Nasdaq mega-cap revenue guidance (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL China exposure).

factors.name: Geopolitical Uncertainty Unresolved
factors.summary: US-Iran stalemate continues into 12th week with no progress; Trump threats, drone attacks on UAE/Saudi, Hormuz tolls escalating. Unresolved tail risk keeps volatility premium elevated and deters positioning in momentum-heavy growth names.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Earnings Catalyst Window Unclear
factors.summary: No major Nasdaq 100 earnings or forward guidance in the immediate ~24-72h window from this news cycle. Hyperscaler capex commentary absent; lack of near-term bullish catalysts in headline flow.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation Negative
factors.summary: Bitcoin sub-$77K, Ether selling pressure from oil surge per Tom Lee's inverse crude-ETH correlation. Tech/crypto linkage suggests risk-off momentum persisting; QQQ/BTC co-movement typically negative in stagflation scenarios.

factors.name: Recent Price Action—Momentum Already Established
factors.summary: NAS100 down -0.67% in 4h, -0.22% in 24h. Move is modest and intraday; not yet extended, but directional bias already set on Friday's rally stall + oil surge.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 opened Week 2 of the Hormuz crisis in a bear trap: Treasury yields spiked on oil-driven inflation fears, real rates rose sharply, and broad equity risk-off kicked in across Asia and Europe. China's economic data miss (retail sales 0.2% vs 2% expected) compounded the weakness, threatening forward guidance from mega-cap exporters (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL). USD strength and unresolved US-Iran stalemate (12 weeks, no progress) keep volatility sticky. The selloff is not yet extended (only -0.67% in 4h), but momentum is downward and confluence of bearish drivers (yields, oil, China, geopolitics) is strong. No major tech earnings or capex catalyst is visible in the next 24–72h to arrest the decline.
catalysts_ahead: G7 Finance Ministers statement (Paris) on bond rout & imbalances—expect rhetoric on fiscal/monetary coordination, could stabilize yields temporarily
Oil price action (WTI $102+) dependent on Hormuz talk progress; any ceasefire hint would flip equities bullish, any escalation deepens selloff
China CPI/PPI data (likely mid-week) for inflation confirmation or surprise
Earnings season tail—Mosaic Q1 miss noted; watch for more guidance revisions citing energy/logistics costs
Samsung strike resolution (47K workers at risk)—if averted, modest relief for chip supply narrative
Fed speakers or Powell remarks on inflation/yields shock—any dovish pivot would be bullish for Nasdaq, hawkish would reinforce selloff
screening.risk_warning: Bias flips BULLISH if: (1) Hormuz closed for extended period is accepted as priced-in; yields stabilize & real rates decline; (2) China stimulus announced or data surprise to upside; (3) Fed speaker signals data-dependent patience, not hikes; (4) Tech mega-cap earnings beat expectations on margin resilience despite macro headwinds. Inverse: if oil spikes above $110 or new Iran escalation, downside accelerates.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early stages, not extended yet. Sell on retest of 29,250–29,300 resistance with yield confirmation (10Y stay >4.3%); tighter stop above 29,400. Wait for pullback to 28,900 support if chasing short for a second-leg target.

factors.name: Treasury Yield Surge
factors.summary: 10Y yields have spiked to multi-year highs (Friday's sharp rise), directly compressing long-duration growth valuations. Bloomberg reports 'ominous bond trades' and yields potentially peaking near 5% in coming weeks, which is structurally hostile to NQ.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: Real yields (TIPS spreads) surged Friday as nominal yields jumped faster than inflation expectations. This is extremely bearish for mega-cap tech's price-to-earnings multiples, which are hypersensitive to real rates above 2.5%.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk: Iran Escalation
factors.summary: Trump's 'clock is ticking' Iran warning on Sunday and drone attack on UAE nuclear plant have reignited war premium. Oil spiked toward $110 Brent, driving risk-off across equities. Global uncertainty is depressing multiple expansion.

factors.name: Oil & Commodity Inflation Shock
factors.summary: Brent crude at $110, IEA warning of rapid commercial inventory depletion in weeks, and potential European jet fuel shortages. This reignites stagflation concerns (growth + inflation), which compress equity valuations and favor bonds.

factors.name: Meta Layoffs & AI Capex Reality Check
factors.summary: Meta announcing 8,000 job cuts this week amid AI spending surge signals cost-cutting after aggressive capex. Strategists (Panmure Liberum) warning that 'big tech capex pullback may rattle markets.' Forward guidance risk is high.

factors.name: China Weakness
factors.summary: China's April retail sales hit 40-month low and investment growth missed. This reduces export demand outlook for US tech and semicon firms, adding headwind to forward earnings revisions.

factors.name: Inflation Surprise Tail Risk
factors.summary: Bloomberg strategists warn 'inflation uptick is sending sell signals to stock bulls' and 'honeymoon period for stocks is over.' No fresh CPI data in this window, but market is re-pricing Fed easing assumptions lower, favoring higher yields.

factors.name: Weak Sentiment & Risk-Off Flows
factors.summary: Markets described as 'cautious and choppy'; crypto down ($672M liquidations, BTC sliding under $77K); ETF exodus reported. NQ futures down 0.4% premarket per Bloomberg, consistent with Friday's sell-off momentum.

factors.name: Bias History Continuity
factors.summary: Your prior reads (2026-05-18 03:43 SELL 7/10 Bearish; 08:03 SELL 6/10) align with sustained downside. No fresh bullish catalyst has overturned the thesis; this is continuation, not reversal.

factors.name: Positive: Selective Tech M&A (NextEra-Dominion, Berkshire Alphabet stake)
factors.summary: NextEra-Dominion $67B power deal and Berkshire's 224% Alphabet increase show mega-cap conviction from smart money, but dwarfed by macro headwinds (yields, oil, China) in current environment.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is in early-stage downtrend continuation following Friday's sharp -0.67% (4h) and -0.22% (24h) declines driven by Treasury yield surge to multi-year highs (5%+ risk), Iran escalation pushing Brent to $110, and forward earnings headwinds from Meta layoffs and China weakness. Real yields rising sharply is the core structural headwind for duration-heavy mega-cap tech. While premarket chop and Iran peace deal rumors (Oman mediation) offer tactical bounces, the macro backdrop (stagflation risk, capex pullback fears, Fed re-pricing toward higher terminal rates) remains hostile. This is Phase 2 of a bearish move — not yet exhausted but move has started.

Fed minutes or next FOMC signal: any hint of 'higher for longer' rates would amplify yield shock
CPI/PCE data (likely late May): fresh inflation surprise would reset Fed easing assumptions and drive yields higher

China stimulus announcements: easing from Beijing could rekindle growth expectations and ease stagflation risk
screening.risk_warning: INVALIDATION: Sharp drop in oil prices (below $105) on Iran peace deal breakthrough, combined with Fed pivot signal (dovish hold or rate-cut timeline moved forward) would collapse real yields and trigger explosive short-cover rally in NQ. Also, unexpectedly strong earnings / rosy AI capex guidance from NVDA/MSFT or hyperscaler commentary could force tactical squeeze higher. Watch for retest of 29,350 intraday resistance; breach would suggest consolidation rather than resumption of downtrend.

Better tactical entry on retest of 29,300–29,350 resistance with confirmed rejection (e.g., multiple taps, bearish candle), or wait for capitulation / capitulation sell-off (VXN spike, NQ -1.5% on day) before fresh shorts. factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield Spike
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield touched highest in a year and 30-year yields at record levels (approaching 5%), per Bloomberg. Inflation shock fears driving global bond selloff. Long-duration growth multiples (NDX's core holding) extremely sensitive to real yield rises.

factors.summary: Roger Altman (Evercore) warned of 'tipping point' in oil market risking $150/barrel, triggering 'second big inflation shock' post-COVID. EU cutting growth, raising inflation in spring forecast. Oil up, Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, energy crunch pressuring capex and AI investment timelines.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations Reset
factors.summary: Ed Yardeni warns incoming Fed Chair Warsh may be forced to raise rates in July vs. expected cuts, per 'bond vigilantes.' This is a hawkish repricing that compresses long-duration valuations central to Nasdaq.

factors.name: Previous SELL Bias (Unconfirmed)
factors.summary: Three prior reads (May 18, 12:29/16:23/16:25 UTC) all flagged SELL 6/10 with 'no confirmation triggered.' Price action now validates underlying weakness; move is developing confirmation.

factors.name: Positive: Zscaler +10%, ServiceNow YTD Best, Berkshire/Delta Positioning
factors.summary: Zscaler jumped 10% on B.Riley upgrade; ServiceNow 'heads for best day in a year' (MarketWatch 17:05). Berkshire added Alphabet +224% and took Delta stake. Some selective strength in software and mega-cap repositioning, but drowned out by macro headwinds.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in PHASE_2 (move underway, down -1.06% in 24h, -0.15% in 4h). The dominant narrative is **rising real yields + Fed hawkish repricing + pre-earnings tech jitters**, compounded by geopolitical risk-off (Iran). Bond vigilantes forcing the Fed's hand; incoming Chair Kevin Warsh may have to HIKE in July despite Trump's dovish mandate (per Yardeni/Fed Chair sworn-in Friday). Memory/chip supply concerns (Seagate, Micron, Western Digital selloff) cast doubt on near-term AI capex returns. Crypto flows (-$1.07B) and late-session equity selling suggest momentum is rolling over. However, conviction is capped at **6/10** because (1) NVDA earnings (this week) are a major unknown, (2) move is already 1%+ down, and (3) credit spreads, while stressed, have not widened catastrophically.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA earnings (this week) — Jensen Huang commentary on China chips, memory constraints, capex outlook will be make-or-break for NDX
Kevin Warsh Fed Chair sworn in Friday — implications for rate trajectory and QT policy
Pending Home Sales (Tuesday) — housing affordability stress test
Fed's Waller speech (Tuesday) — hawkish/dovish tone on inflation, rates
PCE/Core PCE inflation data (likely late week) — critical for real yield repricing
Iran-US tensions escalation/de-escalation — oil price swing implications
FOMC minutes or next meeting guidance — when will hikes resume?
AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL earnings (upcoming) — mega-cap guidance on capex, margins, China exposure
screening.risk_warning: INVALIDATION: (1) NVDA beats and crushes guidance on AI demand + capex confirmation → likely +2-3% pop that overrides macro headwind; (2) Fed Chair Warsh walks back rate-hike expectations, pivots dovish → real yields compress, duration longs rip; (3) Iran ceasefire announced → oil crashes, risk-on resumes, crypto inflows resume; (4) CPI/PCE surprise COOL significantly → Fed off the hook, bonds rally, equities extend. Watch VXN (Nasdaq vol index) — if it spikes >25, liquidation risk; if it drops <18, capitulation bottom forming.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (down 1% in 24h, sellers controlling late session). DO NOT chase shorts here — wait for a retest of resistance at 28,850–29,000 or a 2-3% intraday bounce before adding SELL exposure. Alternatively, if NVDA earnings disappoint this week, breakout below 25,600 is the next SHORT target. For longs, buying into this weakness makes sense ONLY if Kevin Warsh speech (Friday) signals dovish pivot or NVDA crushes.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Real Rates
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yields climbing near multiyear highs (per MarketWatch/Bloomberg reports). Long-duration tech highly sensitive to real rate compression; rising yields pressure growth multiples directly.

factors.name: Inflation & Fed Policy Shift
factors.summary: Fed's Goolsbee reinforces inflation concerns are 'front of mind' under incoming Chair Warsh; markets pricing 50% rate-hike probability by year-end. Bond market issuing 'warning' on inflation (MarketWatch 18:46). Second inflation shock risk cited by Evercore's Altman if oil approaches $150.

factors.name: Oil & Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Oil volatility surging on Iran tensions (Trump delayed 'scheduled attack'); crude oscillating $102–$105. Japan Finance Minister warns of speculative moves; energy shock could trigger stagflation, crushing growth equities despite initial relief rally.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Earnings & Sentiment
factors.summary: NVDA earnings looming (high-stakes call on China chips, capex guidance); Meta laying off 8,000 (AI cost reality check); Musk lost OpenAI lawsuit, removing founder-level headline risk but reinforcing competitive AI narrative. Mixed earnings outlook.

factors.name: Sector Rotation & Memory Sell-Off
factors.summary: Seagate, Micron, SanDisk, Western Digital tanking on capex hesitancy (CEO 'would take too long' to build factories). Semiconductor supply chain weakness; memory chip demand softening. Broadens sector weakness beyond pure software/hyperscalers.

factors.name: Nasdaq 100 Price Action
factors.summary: Down 1.14% intraday to 25,925 session low (per 18:33 report). S&P 500 down 0.60%. Tech bleeding harder than broad market. Prior three SELL6/10 calls unconfirmed; now seeing actual breakdown.

factors.name: Flow & Positioning Risk
factors.summary: Citadel Securities' Rubner warns of 'risk of flow unwind' in US stocks that drove recent record highs. Crypto funds saw $1B outflows on Iran/inflation fears. Dealer gamma and fund positioning increasingly crowded long.

factors.name: Fed Chair Warsh Swearing-In
factors.summary: Warsh sworn in Friday at White House; hawkish inflation rhetoric already priced in. No new surprise expected, but market may re-test resolve into ceremony.

factors.name: Dollar Index
factors.summary: USD correcting lower on Iran tensions easing, but real yields supporting bids. Mixed near-term; longer-term dollar remains underpinned by rate differentials vs. peers.

factors.name: Valuation Bubble Concerns
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports 'Four market sectors look frothy' and S&P 500 has '30% crash probability over 2 years.' NVDA not even biggest bubble. Sentiment stretched; rotation risk acute.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 has rolled over into a bearish phase driven by surging Treasury yields, Fed rate-hike chatter, and multi-front inflation concerns (oil volatility, Middle East risks). Mega-cap earnings season is beginning (NVDA critical), but semiconductor supply concerns (Seagate, memory) and warnings of positioning unwind are weighing. The index is down 1.14% intraday with no confirmation of a bounce; prior three SELL signals remain unconfirmed, but current tape shows teeth in the selling. Risk/reward tilts bearish into FOMC and Warsh swearing-in Friday.
catalysts_ahead: NVIDIA earnings (likely this week) — capex guidance & China commentary critical
Fed Chair Kevin Warsh swearing-in Friday at White House — may re-test hawkish messaging
PCE/Core PCE inflation data (timing TBD, but critical for rate path)
Treasury auction supply & 10-year yield reaction
Iran negotiations update — oil shock tail risk
Meta earnings follow-up after 8k layoff announcement
US-China trade/chip export controls (Xi summit fallout)
screening.risk_warning: If oil tensions ease definitively (Iran deal announced) and Treasury yields roll over on Fed 'patience' rhetoric, a short-squeeze rally back above 29,500 is possible within 24–48h. NVDA blowout earnings on capex/demand could also short-circuit bearish thesis. Conversely, CPI surprise or further geopolitical escalation could accelerate downside below 28,500.
screening.entry_guidance: Phase 2 breakdown underway; avoid chasing shorts here. Wait for retest of 29,200–29,400 resistance on any bounces, or support hold test at 28,500 before committing. NVDA earnings is binary; recommend sidelines or very tight stops until clarity post-call.
